1947.name: A Stone Basement
1947.description: All at once you hear loud scuttling sounds coming from every direction, yet no matter how fast you swivel your head you cannot locate their source. The stone walls of this basement are shrouded in darkness and covered in silky spider webs, giving you a pretty clear mental picture of what might be scraping around down here. You are getting hungry.

npcs.name: Venomous spider
npcs.description: which surely can't be a good sign.  It scurries towards you with surprising

1948.name: A Fruit Cellar
1948.description: The former use of this room is immediately aparent, as foodstuffs that have been decaying for ages fill shelves that line the stone walls of this cellar. The smell is putrid, and it takes nearly all of your willpower just to hang on to your last meal. Thin spider webs span the room, and you cannot take a single step without another breaking on you.

1949.name: A Small Hallway
1949.description: You find yourself in a narrow hallway, and immediately begin to feel claustrophobic. Spider webs coat every surface, including the floor. They make a terrible sound as your boots come down on them, and send countless tiny spiders scurrying up your legs as you slide across the silky webs. You reach out a hand to steady yourself against the wall, and a feeling of pure revulsion fills you as you get a handful of web.

1950.name: The Spider Den
1950.description: Silky spider webs cover the walls of this room, ghostly wisps threatening to trap you for the rest of your short life. A number of large, silk cocoons hang around the room, and they look quite flimsy. Hanging at irregular intervals in the webs are the decaying and completely drained remains of the spiders' previous victims. Surely you will soon be joining them.

npcs.name: Undead spider
npcs.description: places, and its eyes have been dead for a very long time.  Its legs and fangs do not share that condition however.

1944.name: Southern Plains Road
1944.description: The southern plains road runs straight to the north and to the south, here. Passage is featureless save for the occasional bit of rusted armor or blade tossed to the side, and the burrowing holes of small rodents.

npcs.name: Vulture
npcs.description: Ruffled and unkempt, the vulture eyes you keenly as you pass through

1945.name: Southern Plains Road
1945.description: On and on. The road steadily maintains its unbent course. To the east, a shallow river winds its way closer and closer to the road as it progresses southward. Here and there you pass the decaying remnants of roadside structures, long abandoned. One appears safe enough to enter just east of here.

1946.name: An Ancient Entryway
1946.description: The wall of this ancient structure has been torn down in more places than not. What's left of the roof lies in piles of rubble on the ground, crunching beneath your feet with every step and leaving the sky exposed. Various bits of furniture lie smashed about the room, though it is impossible to discern what might have once decorated this space.

1951.name: Southern Plains Road
1951.description: A pile of skeletal remains mark this otherwise featureless stretch of the long southern plains road. Small amphibians skitter out from brush growing near a shallow streaming river just eastward. A thin wisp of smoke rises from hills in the far distance, to the southeast.

1952.name: Southern Plains Road
1952.description: An expanse of grain wavers in the breeze across the plains here as the road turns, offering passage to the north and to the east. The features of an arched bridge over the nearby river are now clearly visible.

1953.name: Southern Plains Road
1953.description: The road descends slightly toward a shallow riverbed, which is spanned by a bridge of worn ebon stonework. A footpath leads east toward the underside of the bridge, and the road turns here offering passage to the south and to the west.

1954.name: Beside Blackstone Bridge
1954.description: Pebbles and brush collect upon the banks of a shallow streaming river, its current running decidedly southward. The water is shallow enough to afford passage beneath the bridge to the southeast.

1955.name: Beneath Blackstone Bridge
1955.description: The air cools by several degrees here beneath the blackstone bridge to Cormanthus. The streaming water surges around your ankles on its course to the south, however small islands of pebble offer ample space to relax here. A thin layer of moss and lichen cover the underside of the bridge. Footpaths along the banks lead to the northwest and the southeast.

npcs.name: Mangy hound
npcs.description: bristling up on it's stout neck.  It bares blackened, broken teeth as

npcs.name: Undead drifter
npcs.description: eyes no longer reflect any of the wonders he has seen.  His soulless husk is now trapped here, just like the rest of the damned.

1956.name: Beside Blackstone Bridge
1956.description: Pebbles and brush collect upon the banks of a shallow streaming river, its current running decidedly southward. To the east the road through the ruins of Cormanthus begins. A footpath leads to the northwest, under the bridge.

1957.name: The Road to Cormanthus
1957.description: You stand upon the road to the ruins of Cormanthus, next to an ebon stone bridge spanning a shallow river to the west. The ruins of roadside structures decaying remains of many a creature and adventurer.

1958.name: The Road to Cormanthus
1958.description: A footpath treads away from the road, westward across the riverbank and under the bridge. The road through the ruins of Cormanthus goes south from here, and northwest across the bridge itself. Hills roll in the distance further eastward.

npcs.name: Cormanth guard
npcs.description: buzz around his head, where a flaming sigil; a stylized F rune, flickers

1959.name: The Road to Cormanthus
1959.description: Bones protrude from the hard, dry earth along the roadside through the ruins of this ancient city's outskirts. The road here turns, leading north and east. To the south, a modest copse of trees rises up along the widening river's banks.

1960.name: Road through Cormanthus
1960.description: A narrow road winds its way through the ruins. The occasional structure appears to have been partially restored and may be inhabited. Rats scurry about searching for sustenance. The trickle of water is heard from a nearby fountain.

1961.name: Road through Cormanthus
1961.description: Dry brush clings to piles of worn stonework from collapsed structures. A few have been pilloried to restore a building to the south, from which steam and smoke erupt along with the sounds of smithing. The road through Cormanthus passes to the northwest and to the east, here, toward an old village square. A cormanth guard shambles in from the south.

npcs.name: Zombie
npcs.description: looks like.  The tattered remains of donated leather goods hang from the ragged humanoid form.  Its eyes dart and its shoulders twitch in short,

1962.name: Cormanthus Blacksmith's Shop
1962.description: Piles of raw ore, presumably mined from the hills northeast of the ruins, lay here along a wall. Stones liberated from other structures have been brough in here to form makeshift worksurfaces, along with a set of enclosures for the raging fire pits toward the rear of the smithy. Cauldrons boil, workers toil, and the implements of destruction are made.

npcs.name: Brogus the Master Smith
npcs.description: a thousand-yard stare.  He seems to look right through you, and often twitches as if responding to instructions from someone or something

1963.name: Road through Cormanthus
1963.description: You pass a series of crumbled stone dwellings along a narrow road through the remains of the village. Westward a structure has been partially restored, and belches smoke. The road turns around a thicket of brush here after proceeding further eastward.

1964.name: Road through Cormanthus
1964.description: Amongst overgrown stone ruins, a road splits. One fork offers passage to the northeast toward a series of rolling hills, while the other meanders southward, toward a large central village square dominated by a fountain. In the distance to the west, a small structure emits smoke, and further off, an arched bridge can be seen.

1965.name: Road through Cormanthus
1965.description: Uniquely, the structure to the north has survived the ravages of time and what calamity may have befallen this village, and appears to be a servicable Inn. The road through the ruins ventures southwest and further eastward here.

1966.name: Cormanthus Inn
1966.description: Inexplicably preserved is the comfortable inn of the village's northern estate. Dozens of cots with separating curtains line a sleeping hall to one side, while a simply appointed meeting room occupies the other. A series of winding stone steps lead downward to a vault, and above, several gargoyles monitor the infrequent visitors.

npcs.name: Helene the Innkeeper
npcs.description: a simple grin she flashes sharpened incisors.  Her beauty could only be skin deep.  There's something very strangely alive... and yet dead, about her.

1967.name: North Cormanthus Road
1967.description: Loose stones are strewn across a narrow winding road beneath the foothills east of the ruined village. Towards the hills, the road gives way to a simple trail, while a few intact structures can be discerned among the ruins to the west and southwest.

1968.name: North Cormanthus Trail
1968.description: A simple trail winds down from the hills and into the ruins of an ancient village. Rough-cut stonework litters the area, along with piles of splintered and snapped bones and rusted armor. The trail widens to become a road to the southwest. Upon the hills to the northeast, a small set of additional ruins apart from the village, are barely visible.

npcs.name: Cormanth trailguard
npcs.description: you with a cold stare.  Death has not strayed him from his post.

1969.name: South Cormanthus Road
1969.description: You must step over the remains of a tremendous winged beast to cross to the north or to the south here, near the center of the village ruins. The skeletal remains mix with dozens of rusted blades and crumbled stones from unrecognizable structures.

1970.name: South Cormanthus Road
1970.description: The skeletal tail of a great winged beast curls up and over an arched doorway into a mostly-intact building to the south. The road through the ruins turns here offering passage north and to the east, toward a large fountain.

1971.name: Cormanthus Village Square
1971.description: Two roads through the ruins of Cormanthus meet here at the remains of the village square. The remnants of a low stone wall are strewn about the edges of the square, and footpaths lead across from several directions. A lightly wooded riparian copse can be seen to the south, while a series of rolling hills arise to the east and northeast.

1972.name: Cormanthus General Supplies
1972.description: Planks of wood and filed stone form makeshift stock shelving for travelling and survival supplies of all sorts here within a modestly restored ruin. The entrance is notably framed by the curling skeletal tail of a large, winged beast, its remains stretched out in the road to the north. The odor of burlap and oil permeates the structure.

1973.name: South Cormanthus Road
1973.description: The road south of Cormanthus twists through a lightly wooded copse near a shallow river. To the north, smoke belches forth from a restored stone ruin within the remains of the village. Pale wildflowers on long stalks rise from mounds along the roadside here. You are getting thirsty.

1974.name: South Cormanthus Road
1974.description: Overgrown with brush fed by a river passing to the west, the road deteriorates into no more than a trail. Tall, thin trees with scant leafage rise above. The area is windless and preternaturally calm, nearly devoid of movement.

1982.name: East Cormanthus Road
1982.description: A narrow road ventures northeast and eastward from the remnants of the Cormanthus village square to the southwest. Crumbled stonework gives way to uncut stone as the road progresses toward rolling hills in the east.

1983.name: East Cormanthus Road
1983.description: A solitary outpost's remains slowly crumble into dust and pebble here, on the eastern outskirts of the ruins. At the base of a small hill a trail picks up where the road leaves off, offering passage northeast. The road turns back toward the ruins to the west.
